title: Calandrinia minima
intro: Calandrinia minima, also known as red maire or red rock purslane, is a perennial succulent plant that belongs to the family Montiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Calandrinia minima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat ailments such as digestive issues and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Calandrinia minima is classified within the family Portulacaceae, but it has also been placed in the family Montiaceae. It belongs to the genus Calandrinia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The species Calandrinia minima is also known as the dwarf portulaca or small purslane. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its small size and succulent leaves. The classification of Calandrinia minima may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of plant taxonomy.
